The Climb · Zodi Karma

Zodi Karma is the merit your animal gathers: the daily visit, the proverbs studied, the moons checked, the matches tested. It buys nothing. It only lifts you, station by station, from Wanderer to Blessed.

  • ✦Blessed by the Gods of Zodi100,000
  • ◉Awakened10,000
  • ·Wanderer0

Six stations in all — the full ladder lives on the Karmic Board.

聚 Capybara
Your Readingabout 6 min Meet the Animal The Crossing Your Element The Primal Mirror The Awakened Animal Your Lifeabout 5 min Working Nature Ideal Habitat Growth Go Deeperbrowse Moon Rhythm Your Proverb Your Peopleplay Relationships Related Animals The Bond Test

0 of 13 chapters read

♋ Cancer and 猪 Year of the Pig

Capybara

You draw a crowd the way a riverbank does, without effort and because the temperature there is right.

Zodi Animal · No. 048 / 144

In brief

Your Primal Zodiac Animal is the Capybara: Cancer's instinct to shelter crossed with the Pig's abundance of warmth.

  • Cancer

    Builds shelter from warmth rather than walls, and holds it open for anyone who needs it.

  • The Pig

    Moves through the world with a generosity that others mistake for calculation.

  • The Capybara

    Becomes the riverbank — the place everything naturally slows down, lands, and stays.

One strength, one cost: you become the ground others organize around — and you can disappear into that role before you have named it.

Meet the Capybara

An animal that lets other species perch on its back, and never notices because it was never performing.

The capybara is the world's largest rodent — a semi-aquatic mammal of South America's grasslands, floodplains, and riverbanks — that lives in groups of ten to a hundred individuals and is, by most measures, the most socially affiliative large mammal on the continent.

Every symbolic reading below comes from these facts. The high-set senses that allow complete submersion while staying alert, the groups so calm that other species perch on them as furniture, the constant warm vocalizations that hold the group together across distance — these are what the archetype is built on. If the biology did not support the claim, the claim was cut.

Consider what it means to be built this way, and what it asks of the person built the same.

6 traits below

01of six

The high waterline

Animal fact

The capybara's eyes, ears, and nostrils are positioned at the very top of the skull, allowing the animal to submerge its entire body while keeping all three senses above the waterline. It can remain submerged for up to five minutes.

Our reading

You have learned to take in enormous amounts of what is happening around you without appearing to be doing so. People often discover, after the fact, that you were reading the room long before they realized it needed reading.

02of six

The most affiliative mammal

Animal fact

Capybaras live in groups of 10 to 100 individuals and regularly tolerate other species — including birds, monkeys, and small predators — resting on and around their bodies. They are described by biologists as among the most affiliative mammals observed in the wild.

Our reading

You did not decide to be the gathering place. Others simply arrived, found the temperature suitable, and stayed. You noticed this was happening long before you found a name for it.

03of six

Constant group vocalizations

Animal fact

Capybaras maintain near-continuous acoustic contact within their groups through a repertoire of vocalizations including purrs, whistles, clicks, and barks, each serving specific social functions such as alarm, contentment, or contact maintenance.

Our reading

Your presence in a group is not decorative. You are producing the warmth that holds it together — the check-in, the laugh that releases tension, the word that names what everyone was waiting to name. You do this so naturally that you rarely receive credit for it.

04of six

Water as the first response

Animal fact

When threatened, the capybara's primary defense is immersion rather than combat or flight. Groups enter water and remain submerged with only their nostrils above the surface until the threat has passed.

Our reading

Your instinct when the world asks too much is to go somewhere calmer — not to fight, not to perform composure, but to become genuinely unavailable for a while. People who do not know you well mistake this for passivity. It is not passivity. It is knowing when to stop being the bank.

05of six

The morillo

Animal fact

Male capybaras possess a morillo — a large, hairless sebaceous gland on the top of the nose — used to scent-mark vegetation and establish territorial presence without confrontation.

Our reading

You claim space quietly, through presence rather than declaration. By the time anyone notices you have made this place yours, you have been here long enough that it is simply true.

06of six

The size that creates safety

Animal fact

The capybara is the world's largest rodent, reaching up to 65 kilograms and 1.3 metres in length. Its size is itself a social resource: other species shelter in its proximity, and predators are less likely to approach a group when the capybara is visible and calm.

Our reading

Something about your presence makes the people around you feel safer than they did before you arrived. You rarely know you are doing this. You are not performing protection. You are simply large enough, warm enough, and steady enough that others reorganize around you.

The Capybara dossier

The Dossier

Zodi Animal
No. 048 / 144

☼ Capybara · Cancer × Pig

The Common Ground

Draws without recruiting. Holds without gripping. Becomes the place others call home.

Profile

Archetype
The Common Ground
Central gift
Ease that invites others in
Central shadow
Absorbing too much of others
Protective instinct
Staying warm, staying near water
Growth lesson
Knowing when the bank can recede
Power phase
Full Moon
Element
Water
Ideal habitat
Open, warm, near water, never empty

These five traits map where this nature operates at its clearest, and where it costs the most. Test yourself against them.

Take the personality test

Tap a trait to see it in yourself

    CapybaraPick a trait above to see how strongly it tends to show up in you, and which way it leans.

    Two zodiacs, one animal

    Where Cancer and the Pig meet

    Cancer brings

    • The instinct to shelter — to make a space that holds people rather than impresses them
    • A memory for how people felt, not just what they said
    • Sensitivity to when the group is off and a quiet effort to bring it back

    The Pig brings

    • An abundance of warmth that arrives without calculation
    • Genuine pleasure in the company of others, without needing to be central to it
    • Generosity that gives more than it tracks

    What the crossing makes

    The Capybara at full function is the gathering point that does not know it is one. Cancer's shelter-building meets the Pig's unguarded warmth and produces someone who does not recruit, does not perform, and does not manage — they simply exist in a way that makes others want to stay. The crossing works because neither sign is competitive. Both move toward warmth, toward holding, toward keeping the group together.

    Where they pull against each other

    Cancer builds home from attachment — it remembers, it tends, it notices when someone is missing. The Pig's warmth is broader, less particular, more easily given without tracking who has taken. In the Capybara, these two can pull: the Cancer half wants to know who is really there, while the Pig half keeps extending the welcome without asking. The reading lives in learning when to count the herd.

    Drag the dial to weigh your two halves and watch the blend change

    Cancer
    Pig
    50 Cancer In balance Pig 50

    In balance: you are easy to find and hard to leave. The Cancer half remembers; the Pig half keeps the door open. You do not recruit, but you do not turn people away. This is the Capybara operating at its steadiest.

    Your element

    水

    Your element is Water

    Water's archetype: The Sage · in the Capybara: The Warm Current

    Water's archetype in this system is the Sage. In the Capybara it becomes something more specific: the Warm Current — a depth that moves toward others rather than away from them. Water at its best is the element that holds everything without demanding shape of its own — adaptive, sustaining, capable of filling any vessel it encounters. In excess, Water loses its banks. It spreads into everything it touches, unable to locate its own edges. The shadow of the Capybara wears this signature: a warmth so pervasive it no longer knows where it stops.

    How this was calculated

    Every Zodi Animal takes its element from the fixed element of its Chinese year-animal: for the Pig, that is Water. Your Western sign adds a second note: Cancer also carries Water. This is a double-Water crossing — both signs run on the same element, which compresses rather than mixes. The Capybara carries undiluted Water. Metal generates Water in the five-phase cycle, keeping this nature replenished; Earth banks Water, giving it a shore. The pairing of animal and element archetype is Zodi Animal's own reading of these traditional systems, offered for reflection.

    DirectionNorth
    SeasonWinter
    VirtueWisdom
    ColorBlack

    The elements around you

    Wu Xing 五行, the Five Phases, is a system of relationships, not isolated traits. Here is how the other four move around yours.

    Generates you

    金Metal clarifies and replenishes

    Metal generates Water in the five-phase cycle: the condensation that forms on cold stone, the mineral sharpness that keeps the current clean. For someone built like the Capybara, Metal energy — precision, structure, clarity — is what restores rather than depletes. Time with detail-oriented people, or with systems that have clear edges, tends to leave you more fluid, not less.

    Balances you

    土Earth contains and shapes

    Earth checks Water in the five-phase cycle: the bank that gives the river its form. Without Earth, Water has no shores and flows everywhere at once. The Capybara's balance comes from the Earth energy in its life — the people, structures, and commitments that say: this far, and here is the bank. Earth does not reduce you. It makes you a river instead of a flood.

    You generate

    木Water feeds growth

    Water generates Wood in the five-phase cycle: the current that carries nutrients to the root. The Capybara naturally sustains growth in others — in creative people, in developing relationships, in anything that needs ongoing nourishment to reach its full form. You feed things without meaning to. The question is whether you are doing it consciously.

    You restrain

    火Water cools what burns

    Water restrains Fire in the five-phase cycle: the current that keeps the system from consuming itself. The Capybara moderates intensity in any group it is part of — bringing temperature down when things run hot, absorbing volatility before it becomes damage. This is a genuine gift. It is also the one most likely to leave you carrying the heat afterward.

    Go deeper: Bazi 八字

    This is Wu Xing 五行, and it runs deeper

    The element you carry here comes from the fixed element of the Year of the Pig — a starting note, not the full chart. Wu Xing reads the broad current; the Four Pillars of Destiny (Bazi) maps the hour, day, month, and year of birth together, producing a picture specific enough to hold the weight of a single life.

    If the Capybara reading is landing for you, the Four Pillars may show you precisely why — which pillars reinforce the Water, and which introduce the friction that the broad reading can only name without explaining.

    BaziRead your Four Pillars ElementsSee all five phases

    Go deeper: Chakras

    Water in the body

    Held beside the yogic map, Water finds its closest match in the Sacral chakra, Svadhisthana. It governs flow, feeling, and the quality of how you move through and with others — in the Capybara, this is the current that keeps the gathering warm. Its bija sound is VAM; its element there is water.

    Treat the pairing as an interpretive bridge between two traditions, not an equivalence.

    Sacral chakraSvadhisthanaBija: VAMElement: water
    ChakraVisit your Sacral chakra PracticeYoga for your element

    Take it further

    水ElementThe Water element in full OverviewAll five elements Bazi · 八字Your Four Pillars Feng shuiSet up your workspace

    The Primal Mirror

    Every reading passes five gates, in order

    One mechanism, five states. Reflex, capacity, defense, distortion, integration.

    Swipe the gates · tap to open each one

    01 · Reflex

    The Bank Before You Know It

    You make room for people before you have decided whether to.

    When someone arrives — at the table, in the conversation, into a moment of difficulty — your first movement is toward them. Not dramatically. Not with an announcement. The water simply becomes warmer in that direction. You adjust the group without calling attention to what you are doing, and by the time the adjustment is complete, it looks as though it was always this way.

    The capybara does not convene its herd. It simply exists in a place, with a quality that makes the herd cohere around it. Other species arrive without being invited. No mechanism is visible. The bank is warm; things collect there.

    02 · Capacity

    Where Everything Slows Down

    You are the place people come when they need to stop performing.

    At its fullest, this nature produces something that is difficult to manufacture and impossible to fake: a quality of presence that gives other people permission to set something down. They stop explaining themselves around you. They stop holding their faces in the shape the day has been asking for. They arrive at the bank and simply remain.

    Groups you are part of tend to function better than they did before you joined them — not because you introduced a system, but because you introduced a temperature. You often do not receive credit for this because what you do is not visibly a contribution. It is more like a condition.

    03 · Defense

    What the Warmth Is For

    The bank exists because you learned early that warmth is what keeps the herd together.

    The openness is not naivety. It is a conclusion reached over time: that the group holds, and that what holds the group is the quality of the warmth at its center. You maintain the warmth because you have seen what happens when it drops — when the bank cools, the herd fragments, and the predators approach from three directions at once.

    What you are protecting is not any one person but the condition itself. The gathering. The cohesion. The fact that when someone in the group needs somewhere to land, there is still somewhere.

    04 · Distortion

    When the Bank Cannot Recede

    You can disappear into the role of gathering place and not notice until the water has been running for a very long time.

    What trips it. Any signal that the group needs tending: someone withdrawing, a tension in the room, a friend who seems less warm. The instinct fires before you have assessed whether it is yours to fix.

    What your mind says. If I stay warm, this will resolve. If I stay available, everyone will be all right.

    What you do. You remain. You extend. You absorb the weather of the room and work to bring the temperature up. You do not claim anything as yours. You do not leave the water.

    What it costs. You begin to carry the moods of everyone around you and cannot locate your own. Other people's needs have a clear address; yours have become <b>the shoreline others walk on without realizing it is you</b>.

    In love

    You absorb a partner's distress so completely that by the time they feel better, you have taken on what they put down. They feel relieved. You are carrying something that was never yours and cannot name it.

    At work

    You keep the temperature of the team stable through difficult stretches and are never asked how you are doing, because you have never looked like you needed it.

    With friends

    You are the one everyone calls when the group needs holding together. You are rarely the one someone calls to ask how you are spending the afternoon.

    05 · Integration

    Awakened: the same strength, integrated

    You still make room for people before you have decided whether to — and now you sometimes decide not to.

    The gathering place does not disappear. The warmth does not switch off. What changes is that you develop a relationship with the water level — you learn that rivers recede, that banks are not always available, that the capacity to hold is larger when the bank has also been allowed to dry in the sun sometimes. You begin to treat your openness as a real resource rather than an infinite one.

    The herd still coheres around you. But you have begun to count who is in it.

    Name the form you are becoming

    Complete the sentence: I hold warmth for others, and I also…

    Beyond the reading · ✦ Purple Star

    Meet the awakened Capybara

    The Mirror showed you the awakened form in outline. Purple Star astrology, Zi Wei Dou Shu (紫微斗数), is the oldest and most detailed of the Chinese chart systems, and it maps the Capybara you are becoming, not only the one you are.

    Keep reading it, in your dashboard

    A free account saves your chart and brings a new reading for your animal each week.

    紫微

    Your full Purple Star chart

    All fourteen stars across your twelve palaces, cast for your Cancer Pig crossing and saved to your dashboard.

     ☼

    The awakened Capybara, tracked

    Your path from the fallen form to the awakened one, followed over time, with a fresh reading for your animal every week.
    縁

    The karmic fortune of five

    Invite five friends to find their Zodi Animal, and your Zodi Karma opens the Circle reading: how the six of you move together.

    Create your free account

    Free. No card. Your chart, your weekly reading, and your invited circle, kept in one place.

    Career and working life

    Your working nature

    An application of the archetype, not a verdict: you are likely to work best when the environment runs on warmth and the work involves holding people together across distance or difficulty.

    01 / 05

    The Temperature of the Room

    You do not lead through title or volume. You lead through the quality you create in a group — the ease that makes others willing to contribute, the warmth that makes difficult conversations possible, the presence that holds a team together through long, unglamorous stretches. Your teams often function well without being able to explain why, and they notice your absence more than your presence.

    The challenge of this style: it is invisible, so it is rarely credited. The people above you may not know that what you do is leadership. They see the temperature of the room; they do not see who is holding it.

    02 / 05

    What Lets the Bank Stay Full

    You work best in environments that value cohesion as much as output — teams where relationship is understood to be infrastructure, not distraction. You need enough autonomy to tend the group at your own pace, and enough warmth in the environment to replenish what you give.

    What drains you: highly competitive environments where warmth is read as weakness, situations where you are asked to perform emotion on a schedule, roles that require constant visibility without room to submerge. You are not built for the spotlight. You are built for the riverbank.

    • Collaborative over competitive
    • Relationship-forward roles
    • Sustained rather than sprint work
    • Environments with actual warmth

    03 / 05

    The Decision That Takes Too Long

    You tend to hold decisions open until you have registered how each person in the group will be affected. This produces thorough outcomes and slow timelines. The practice that corrects it: identify the one person most affected and decide for their benefit first. The group will feel the care in the outcome even if it did not feel the deliberation.

    Delegation is genuinely easy for you — you trust others and you mean it. The pattern to watch: you sometimes delegate everything that asks you to be visible and keep for yourself everything that asks you to be useful. Over time this leaves you indispensable and invisible in equal measure.

    04 / 05

    When the Warmth Runs Out

    The loud failure is conflict avoidance that tips into passivity: you absorb friction rather than naming it, and the friction accumulates until the bank can no longer hold it.

    The quiet failure is more costly: you give so much of your capacity to maintaining the group's temperature that your own work gets thin. The burnout pattern for this animal is not collapse — it is a long, slow lowering of the water level, so gradual that neither you nor anyone around you notices until the bank is dry.

    05 / 05

    What the Riverbank Becomes Over Time

    What compounds for you is trust. Not the fast trust of a charismatic entrance, but the slow accumulation of reliability — the fact that you were there last time, and the time before, and did not make it about yourself. Over years, this produces a depth of relationship that most people in your orbit cannot quite account for. They simply know they trust you and cannot fully explain why.

    One growth practice: once a month, send a message to someone who holds ground for you the way you hold it for others. Name what you notice. It is a small thing. It is also the practice most foreign to someone built like you.

    Your Ideal Habitat

    Living with your Zodi Animal

    A room that has room for more.

    The capybara exists in environments that support both immersion and gathering — open water access, enough shade for a large group, soft ground along the bank. The human translation is a space that holds people without feeling staged for them: warm, open, without hard edges that say this room has an agenda.

    Your feng shui palette

    Feng shui builds a room from your element and the one that feeds it. Water is yours; Metal is what feeds it. For the Capybara, this means the palette of still, deep rivers and the quiet gleam of the stone the current polishes.

    Most of the roomA small accent

    GroundWaterAbout 45% of the room

    Deep river

    The ground of the capybara's world is the water itself — deep, still, carrying the day's warmth after the sun has passed.

    SupportWaterAbout 29% of the room

    Reed shadow

    The green-teal of the riverbank vegetation — the place between the water and the open sky where the group gathers.

    AccentMetalAbout 17% of the room

    River stone

    Metal feeds Water in the five-phase cycle: the cool grey of stones worn smooth by current, which clarify and strengthen the Water surrounding them.

    SparkMetalAbout 9% of the room

    Morning mist

    The soft silver of river mist at first light — Metal's lightest note, providing contrast without hardness.

    Arrange your whole space with feng shui

    Workspace

    The capybara does not function well in isolation — it keeps contact with the herd through sound. Your workspace needs some form of human presence or the noise of a living environment nearby. A closed door and complete silence produce a particular kind of low-grade distress that you may not identify as distress for a long time.

    Bedroom

    Soft surfaces and warmth. The capybara rests on soft grass or in shallow mud, in contact with others. Your bedroom should feel like the inside of something, not the outside — enclosed enough to feel held, warm enough to feel inhabited.

    Entry

    The entry of a capybara's space is the bank — the place where inside and outside meet, where the group assembles and disperses. Make yours genuinely welcoming: not a performance of welcome, but the actual condition of it. Something warm at the threshold. Soft light. The suggestion that more people belong here.

    Living area

    Open configuration over separated seating. The herd does not arrange itself around a focal point; it occupies the bank together. Seating that allows multiple people to be present without anyone feeling like a guest is the capybara's native arrangement.

    Gathering space

    If your home has a table, it should seat more people than you expect to fill it. The capybara's group expands. You likely know this already: someone always ends up joining. Plan for the someone.

    Reflection

    Water, near the place where you start and end the day. A bowl, a small fountain, a view of rain. Not for ceremony — for orientation. The capybara knows where it is by the water. You may find the same is true of you.

    Elements to avoid

    • Spaces that require performance — rooms designed to impress rather than hold
    • Seating that separates or competes rather than collects
    • Complete acoustic isolation — you need the sound of a living environment
    • Hard, cold surfaces without soft counterweights
    The five-minute reset

    Fill a bowl with warm water and set it where you can see it. Sit near something soft. Tell one person in your space — or in your phone — that you are here.

    水 Feng Shui
    for the
    Capybara

    Free with a Zodi Animal account

    Redesign your space with feng shui

    A room-by-room guide tuned to your Water element: arrange your home so it works with your nature instead of against it. Yours free when you make a Zodi Animal account.

    • Room by room, tuned to your Water element
    • What to place, what to clear, and where
    • Free the moment you make an account
    Get the free eBook

    Feng shui elements here are symbolic. They support intention and act as visual reminders, not claims that any object, color, or direction produces wealth, health, romance, or success.

    Integration

    Practices for the Common Ground

    Small, physical, repeatable. One from each room of your life.

    • The lesson

      You are the gathering place. That is real, and it is a genuine gift. The practice is learning that gathering places have capacity — that the bank can only hold so much water before it either floods or recedes. Your growth is not in becoming less warm. It is in learning that the warmth is a real resource, not an infinite one, and in finding out what you need to replenish it.

    • A ritual

      At the Full Moon: go somewhere you can hear water, or fill a bowl. Sit with it. Count, out loud or in writing, the people you are currently holding. Then name one thing you need that is not currently being held for you.

    • In your space

      Place a small bowl of water near where you most often tend others — your kitchen, your desk, wherever you are most reliably found. It marks that place as yours too, not only a service space.

    • In one bond

      Say: "I have been carrying something I have not named yet. Can I tell you what it is?"

    • At work

      Name one difficulty in your team one week earlier than you normally would. Notice whether naming it early changed the temperature of the room, or simply let you out of the work of holding it alone.

    Moon rhythm

    Your power moon

    The Full Moon is the phase of visibility and fullness — when what has been growing reaches the form it has been building toward. For an animal that draws others without trying, the Full Moon names what is already true: the group is visible, the warmth is at peak, the bank is holding. The Capybara's power phase is not the dark gathering time but the bright, full, collective one.

    Your next full moon · Power Moon

    Sunday, August 9, 2026

    29 days from today.

    Clear an evening. Bring a person or two, or make a call. The Full Moon is the condition you were built for — not for solitude, but for the visible warmth of the group at full tide. And then: let the water recede.

    1. Saturday, August 8The night before: gather the people. If you have been tending a group in silence, this is the day to let it be visible. Make a plan, extend the invitation, fill the table.
    2. Sunday, August 9 · Full MoonYour peak: be present with the people you have been holding. Count them — not literally, but feel the weight of the group. Then name one thing you need from it.
    3. Monday, August 10The day after: return to your own water. Let the bank recede slightly. Notice what remains when the gathering disperses — that remainder is yours.
    Explore the Moon hub

    The proverb of your year

    衆zhòng志zhì成chéng城chéng

    Tap a tile to hear one character

    Literally When many wills unite, they form a city wall.

    Meaning Collective will creates something no individual could build alone.

    The capybara does not build the wall. It becomes the ground the wall forms on. You are not the leader who assembles the group; you are the condition the group assembles around. The proverb knows this. The city that many wills make is only possible because somewhere, someone remained — warm and steady and present — long enough for the others to arrive.

    A proverb we tie to the Year of the Pig. The pairing is our own reading, not tradition.

    In relationship

    How the Capybara loves

    You rarely ask people to stay. You rarely have to.

    Care, for the Capybara, is not an event. It is a quality maintained at a low, continuous frequency — the purr that keeps the group cohered, the warmth that keeps the bank full. The pattern underneath it is simple: you believe that if the conditions are right, the people you love will be all right. Your job, as you understand it, is to maintain the conditions.

    How you show love

    By making the environment feel warmer than it was. By being easy to find. By remaining present without announcement.

    What makes you feel safe

    A partner who stays near the water. Who does not require you to perform warmth but simply receives it.

    What they may misunderstand

    That your ease is depth. You are not casual; you are steady. The capybara's bank looks like a resting place. It is also a home.

    Your conflict pattern

    You go quiet and go deeper into the water. You do not argue. You become very calm and very unavailable, and wait for the temperature to return.

    What to practice

    Say: "I need the water to myself for a while. I will come back."

    How you show up

    You are the friend people find themselves calling without knowing exactly why. Your door is the one that stays open when others close.

    What they may misunderstand

    That you are always fine. You have learned to look warm even when the water is cold, and most people take the surface reading.

    What to practice

    Let someone find out you are cold. Let them bring warmth to you for once.

    How you show up

    As the one who holds the family's temperature — who notices when something has shifted, who quietly works to bring things back, who everyone treats as the most stable riverbank in the system.

    What they may misunderstand

    That your availability is not the same as your capacity. The bank can only hold so much water.

    What to practice

    Name one thing you need from the family that you have been managing without.

    How you show up

    As the person who holds the team together through the unglamorous middle of things — who keeps the temperature right when the work is difficult.

    Your conflict pattern

    You absorb friction rather than naming it. By the time you address a difficulty, it has usually been building for longer than anyone realized.

    What to practice

    Name the friction one week earlier than feels comfortable.

    The Capybara needs a specific kind of reciprocity: not grand gestures but simple returns. Someone who notices when the water level has dropped. Who arrives with warmth rather than need, at least some of the time. Who makes the bank feel like it is shared rather than managed.

    Test this bond with a second birth date

    Computed across all 143 other crossings

    Matches the wheel favors

    Strongest pull

    Match I · of three

    木

    Sable Antelope

    ♉ Taurus × 虎 Tiger

    1950, 1962, 1974, 1986, 1998, 2010, 2022

    Secret friendsYour Water feeds their Wood

    The Tiger is the Pig's secret friend, the wheel's quiet allied pair. Your Water feeds their Wood: you are the warmth in it.

    Read the Sable Antelope

    Match II · of three

    木

    Sea Otter

    ♋ Cancer × 兔 Rabbit

    1951, 1963, 1975, 1987, 1999, 2011, 2023

    Same trineYour Water feeds their Wood

    The Rabbit shares the Pig's trine, so effort between you compounds. Your Water feeds their Wood: you are the warmth in it.

    Read the Sea Otter

    Match III · of three

    水

    Star-nosed Mole

    ♍ Virgo × 猪 Pig

    1947, 1959, 1971, 1983, 1995, 2007, 2019

    Same yearShared Water

    Two Pig years run on the same clock. Two Water natures recognize each other on sight.

    Read the Star-nosed Mole

    Stay in the water

    Where to go from the Capybara

    Three matches surfaced above; the wheel holds 140 more. Each crossing opens onto others, and these are meanings to explore, not verdicts. Contrast is a mirror for self-knowledge, never a warning.

    Best next reading

    See how Cancer changes across the other eleven years

    The same shelter-building instinct wearing eleven different builds — the fastest way to feel what Cancer contributes and what the year transforms.

    All Cancer animals

    Animals your instincts trust on sight

    The same warmth that holds without gripping — animals that gather without performing.

    Asian ElephantCancer · Year of the Ox

    The temperaments that stretch yours

    All edges, no warmth by default — the animals that know when to leave the water entirely.

    Honey BadgerAries · Year of the Pig

    Same year, other skies

    The Pig's warmth and abundance expressed through eleven other Western signs — each one a different shape of the same fundamental generosity.

    Honey BadgerAries · Year of the Pig BongoTaurus · Year of the Pig CacomistleGemini · Year of the Pig Golden Snub-nosed MonkeyLeo · Year of the Pig
    Explore all of these crossings
    Star-nosed MoleRoseate SpoonbillDeep Sea AnglerfishTree KangarooGiant ArmadilloPlatypusBlue Glaucus

    Same sky, other years

    Cancer's shelter-building instinct across eleven other year-animals — the same warmth, differently clad.

    Coconut CrabCancer · Year of the Rat Asian ElephantCancer · Year of the Ox Moon BearCancer · Year of the Tiger Sea OtterCancer · Year of the Rabbit

    Step back and see the system

    The hubs this crossing hangs from.

    All Cancer animalsThe Western wing All Pig animalsThe Eastern wing The Water elementShared ground The full MenagerieAll 144 animals

    The last chapter

    The Capybara is yours.
    Now find out who else knows how to let the shore find them.

    The Bond Test

    Bring a friend into the reading

    One birthdate is all it takes. Turn the wheel to their year, and their animal steps out to meet the Capybara.

    Your first friend

    01 The year they were born
    牛 1995 · Year of the Pig
    02 The month
    03 The day

    The Moon tonightCome back at the Full Moon with the people you have been holding — and name what you need. Wander the MenagerieAll 144 crossings. Each one opens onto others.

    The Common Ground · Cancer × Pig · Water 水

    Your line to keep

    I am the Capybara: I draw without recruiting. I hold without gripping. I become the place others call home.

    聚

    Where you go next

    Three doors are open. Pick one.

    You know the shape of the Capybara now. Here is what the reading did not tell you yet, and where each thread runs.

    Bonds

    Test a friend against your wheel

    Drop any name onto the Capybara wheel and see where they sit. Ally, mirror, or friction.

    Metal keeps the water clear; Earth gives the bank its shape.

    Run the Bond Test     Purple Star 紫微

    Your chart is bigger than one animal

    Purple Star astrology, Zi Wei Dou Shu, maps your whole birth chart: fourteen stars across twelve palaces, cast from your exact birth hour.

    The reading here is the outline your Cancer Pig shares. Your own chart shows which palace runs loudest.

    Cast your chart

    Save your Capybara before you go.

    A free account keeps this reading and every animal you test, on any device. Come back to where you left off instead of a blank page.

    About a minute to set up. No card. Delete it anytime.

    Sends it to a friend.

    Capybara · 048 / 144 · Water 水

    Pass it on

    Share your crossing

    Keep every reading you are given.

    Make it permanent

    This device will remember the Capybara until it doesn't. A free account keeps your animal, your reflections, and every reading you are given, renewed with each new moon, on any device.

    • Your Capybara, saved for good
    • Your identity line and keeper stone, kept
    • The feng shui eBook for your element, free
    Create your free account

    The Capybara Test

    Do you draw others without trying, the way the Capybara does?

    The Capybara is built from five traits. Ten quick questions measure yours against its shape and tell you whether you are a true Capybara, or a rare one who carries them differently.

    5 traits 10 questions ≈ 2 minutes No account needed

      Capybara You

      How this reading is built

      Each Zodi Animal reading combines four threads: Western zodiac symbolism (your Sun sign), Chinese zodiac symbolism (your year animal), five-element interpretation (Wu Xing, via the year-animal's fixed element), and the real biology and behavior of the animal itself. The archetype that binds them, here The Common Ground, is Zodi Animal's own framework. Readings are for reflection and self-exploration; they are not clinical assessment, scientific personality testing, or a claim about fate. Animal facts are drawn from zoological references and flagged in the text as Animal fact; interpretive material is flagged as Our reading.

      • IUCN Red List · Hydrochoerus hydrochaeris (Capybara)
      • Herrera, E. A. & Macdonald, D. W. (1993) — Aggression, dominance and mating success among capybara males, Behavioral Ecology
      • Moreira, J. R. & Macdonald, D. W. (1996) — Capybara use of space in a seasonally flooded savanna, Biological Conservation

      Last reviewed 2026-07-11

      Your Readingabout 6 min Meet the AnimalThe CrossingYour ElementThe Primal MirrorThe Awakened Animal Your Lifeabout 5 min Working NatureIdeal HabitatGrowth Go Deeperbrowse Moon RhythmYour Proverb Your Peopleplay RelationshipsRelated AnimalsThe Bond Test

      The Primal Zodiac, 144 animals

      Zodi Animal

      One hundred and forty-four animals, born where the Western Sun signs cross the Chinese zodiac years. A slow tradition of meaning, kept under the moon.

      Find your Zodi Animal ✦

      The Daily Reading

      Your reading, at dawn.

      One short passage for your Zodi Animal each morning. The moon's phase, what it favors, and a line meant for you. Free, and it takes ten seconds to join.

      No noise. Unsubscribe anytime.
